name: knowledge-agent
tagline: Builds and queries filtered 'knowledge corpora' (brains) from claude-mem observation history via a build->prime->query workflow.
verdict: Clean, narrow-scope skill: it only orchestrates the plugin's own build_corpus/prime_corpus/query_corpus/list_corpora/rebuild_corpus/reprime_corpus commands over local observation data, with no shell execution, network calls, secrets access, or hidden instructions. Minor rough edges for a user relying on it: no guidance on what happens if a build/query fails

highlights.summary: No negative-trigger guidance anywhere in the file; a user can't tell from the skill when NOT to reach for it (e.g. one-off lookups vs. building a persistent corpus).
Describes side effects loosely ('creates an AI session', 'answers from its corpus') but never specifies the actual output format/shape a caller should expect from query_corpus or list_corpora.
No guidance for failure paths: what happens if build_corpus finds zero matching observations, if prime_corpus is called on a nonexistent corpus, or a name collision occurs.
